briefs.title: Burnaby updates how it enforces bylaw notices — rules now officially changed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has formally passed Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026, updating the rules around how bylaw notices (official warnings or fines from the city) are issued and enforced.
briefs.action_required: Check with Burnaby's bylaw office to confirm how notice delivery or dispute rules may affect your business.
briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: 190 residents petition against rezoning application at 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council received a petition signed by 190 residents opposing rezoning application REZ #25-13 at 5058 Imperial Street. The petition was received for information only — no decision on the rezoning has been made yet.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meeting agendas for a hearing or decision on REZ #25-13 at 5058 Imperial Street.

briefs.title: Burnaby parking meter and EV charging meter bylaw amendment now in effect
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has given final approval to an amendment updating the rules for parking meters and electric vehicle (EV) charging meters across the city.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business operates parking or EV charging meters in Burnaby and review the updated bylaw requirements.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby raises fees and charges: Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026 now in force
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026, updating the city's Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This means some fees the city charges for permits, licences, or services may have changed.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule to see if any permits or licences you pay for have changed in cost.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Zoning change at 6800 Lougheed Hwy moves forward after first two council readings
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council passed the first two readings of a zoning bylaw amendment for the property at 6800 Lougheed Highway, moving it closer to approval. More readings or a public hearing are still required before the change becomes final.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates at or near 6800 Lougheed Hwy, monitor upcoming council meetings for the final reading and public hearing date.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Zoning change approved for 2209–2225 Springer Avenue — development rules may shift
briefs.summary: City council gave first and second reading (early approval steps) to a zoning bylaw amendment for properties at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. This means the permitted land uses or building rules for that site are being changed.
briefs.action_required: If you own, lease, or plan to develop property at 2209–2225 Springer Avenue, contact the city to confirm how the new zoning affects you.

briefs.title: Zoning change at 5058 Imperial Street passes first two readings at Burnaby Council
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to land-use rules) for the property at 5058 Imperial Street. Four council members voted against it.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor future council readings for final approval.

briefs.title: City Council reviews 2025 investment performance and 2026 financial forecast
briefs.summary: City Council received a report on how its 2025 investment program performed and what financial returns are expected in 2026. No policy changes or new rules were introduced — this was an information-only update.

briefs.title: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan project officially initiated
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has received the project initiation for the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan — a long-term blueprint guiding how land is used and developed across the city.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby's OCP process for upcoming public consultations or zoning changes that may affect your property or business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 12-Storey, 290-Unit Mixed-Use Building Approved for 6535 Kingsway in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (a formal rule change that allows a new type of building on a property) for a 12-storey development at 6535 Kingsway, adding 290 homes and ground-floor commercial space.
briefs.action_required: If you operate near 6535 Kingsway, monitor the rezoning bylaw process for construction impacts and new commercial tenancy opportunities.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Five-Storey Self-Storage Facility at 2757 Norland Avenue Approved for Rezoning
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw for a five-storey enclosed self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. No public hearing is required because the project aligns with the city's Official Community Plan (the master land-use guide for the city).
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Poverty Reduction Strategy; implementation plan and provincial funding push underway
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved a Poverty Reduction Strategy and directed staff to build an action plan with community partners. The Mayor will also ask the provincial government for funding to support the effort.

briefs.title: Carrigan Court mixed-use development rezoning application accepted by Council
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has accepted a rezoning application for 3856–3870 Carrigan Court, allowing staff to work with the developer on a high-density project combining commercial space, residential strata (privately owned units), and rental housing.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Council meeting agendas for the public hearing date on this rezoning application.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 3900 Grand Promenade rezoning moves forward: office space swapped for hotel, restaurant, and 362 new homes
briefs.summary: Council approved the first step to rezone 3900 Grand Promenade, replacing some planned office space with a hotel and restaurant, and adding 362 new market condos across two towers. Affordable housing units originally planned for this site will move to a nearby location called Carrigan.
briefs.action_required: Attend upcoming public hearing if your business is near 3900 Grand Promenade or affected by this development.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: 19 hired equipment contracts awarded — payment based on actual quantities delivered
briefs.summary: Council approved contracts with 19 separate equipment suppliers for hired equipment services. Contractors will be paid based on the actual amount of work or materials delivered, not a fixed upfront price.

briefs.title: Municipal asphalt paving contract worth $1.155M renewed with Blackrete Paving
briefs.summary: Council approved a contract renewal with Blackrete Paving Ltd. to provide asphalt paving services for the Engineering Department, valued at $1,155,000 including taxes.

briefs.title: Modular Four-Plex Housing Pilot Contract Sent Back for Efficiency Review
briefs.summary: City council paused the contract award for a modular four-plex housing pilot at 8315 14th Avenue, sending it back to staff to find ways to manage the project more efficiently.

briefs.title: BC municipality approves $36,250.57 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: Council approved paying $36,250.57 (including taxes) in 2026 membership dues to the Union of BC Municipalities, the provincial association that represents local governments.

briefs.title: $4.2M water metering contract awarded to Neptune Technology Group Canada
briefs.summary: A municipal council awarded a $4.2 million contract to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water metering technology, taxes included.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority approves five-year strategic plan to expand affordable housing through 2030
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Burnaby Housing Authority's 2026–2030 strategic plan, setting the direction for the city-owned affordable housing developer for the next five years.
